item.title: 澳大利亚官员要求球迷尊重尼尔的隐私，这只重达一吨的海豹什么也不尊重
item.description:
WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Like plenty of local boys before him, Neil has come home to the stretch of Australian coast where he was born. Unlike most of them, he trails fame, fans and property damage in his wake. He is also a 1,000 kg (2,200 pound) elephant seal.
In June, the bellowing and blubbery 5-year-old mammal hauled himself onto land for his twice-yearly tour of beachside towns in southern Tasmania state after months of feeding at sea. That’s posing problems now that he weighs as much as a small car and has a social media following more than double Tasmania’s human population.
His rampage through local infrastructure has claimed bent traffic bollards, a sign warning the public about seals and a fence that did not survive Neil’s attempt to vault it. The rest of the time he lies placidly any place he likes, which is sometimes the middle of the road, bringing towns he visits to a standstill.
But officials say their biggest concern is that Neil’s popularity could lead to ill-advised human-seal encounters that are dangerous for both sides.
In this photo provided by Sam Volker Photography, Neil the Seal, a 1,000 kg (2,200 pound) elephant seal, moves through an area with traffic cones and bollards in Tasmania, Australia, June 27, 2026. (Sam Volker Photography via AP)
Neil, the only male elephant seal to visit Tasmania in years, has commanded an enthralled TikTok following of 1.4 million in part because he acts like kind of a jerk. During this visit to shore, his 12th, his crimes have included picking fights with parked cars and smashing through barriers erected to keep him off roads.
Those antics have prompted some online to hail Neil as a kind of anti-authoritarian hero. But experts say it’s normal experimentation for a growing seal.
Juvenile male elephant seals need to practice for dominance battles in which adults rear up and crash their chests together as they compete for breeding opportunities, said Sophia Volzke, an elephant seal scientist based at the University of Tasmania in Hobart.
With no other juveniles to practice with, Neil can only rehearse on Toyotas.
In this photo provided by Sam Volker Photography, Neil the Seal, a 1,000 kg (2,200 pound) elephant seal, rests against a sign post as it moves through an area in Tasmania, Australia, on June 27, 2026. (Sam Volker Photography via AP)
Local officials fear that Neil is thelatest wild animalwhose social media stardom has outgrown what’s good for him.
“Neil’s fame is a bit of a double-edged sword,” said Kris Carlyon from Tasmania’s Department of Natural Resources and Environment, at a news conference in Hobart on Thursday in which he asked the seal’s fans to give him privacy.
“We have had some pretty silly behavior, instances with people carrying their small babies up close to him and simply trying to get that shot for Instagram,” he said.
Officials have urged the public to refrain from identifying the town Neil is currently delighting or terrorizing, depending on who you speak to. They fear a disastrous encounter between the seal and an admirer could force rangers into a risky operation to move him elsewhere.
Carlyon also warned of worse. In a 2023 episode, a walrus known as Freya who drew huge crowds in Norwaywas euthanizedafter officials cited a growing risk to human safety.
“There is a risk here of essentially loving Neil to death,” Carlyon said.
In this photo provided by Sam Volker Photography, Neil the Seal, a 1,000 kg (2,200 pound) elephant seal, plays with a traffic cone in Tasmania, Australia, on June 27, 2026. (Sam Volker Photography via AP)
It’s usual for seals to return biannually to the place they were born to rest, fast and shed fur. Many species roam inland during visits to shore, sometimes leading them intobeachside towns.
What’s unusual about Neil is that he’s the only male elephant seal hauling ashore in Tasmania.
Sub-Antarctic islands south of Tasmania are home to breeding populations of elephant seals and Neil’s mother would have arrived from one of them to give birth, Volzke said. Females have been spotted ashore in Tasmania before, but topping out at the size Neil reached when he was a year or two old, they don’t cause the same kind of chaos, she added.
“Humans got rid of those animals and now maybe they are coming back and repopulating areas that they were previously seen in,” she said. “We do need to find a way to coexist.”
That could prove tricky for Neil, and for the rangers, police officers and security guards who follow in his wake. If he survives to adulthood, Neil could measure up to 5 meters (16 feet) in length and weigh triple what he does now.
However, about 90% of male elephant seals die before they reach a breeding age of around 10, Volzke said.
For now, Neil the seal is occupying a stretch of sidewalk, unmoving and unbothered. Sometimes he canoodles with an orange traffic cone, to the delight of his online followers. It isn’t clear why he prefers that location, which he has returned to even after being ushered away by rangers.
“He’s obviously decided this puddle surrounded by bollards, which are horizontal at the moment, is his spot,” said Carlyon on Thursday.
His fans can relate. The locals have mixed feelings.
“He’s one of our biggest exports at the moment,” said Dale Creamer, a resident of the town that the seal is currently trashing, who has not been personally inconvenienced. “It’s Neil’s world and we’re just living in it.”

item.title: 赢得关税减免的比利时钻石集团向特朗普赠送了一枚奢华的镶嵌戒指
item.description:
布鲁塞尔（美联社）——数十颗钻石在星条旗旁边拼出两个巨大的字母“T”以及“1776”和“2026”。还有数十个将数字 45 和 47 框成超人标志的形状。一只长着钻石翅膀的鹰，手持红宝石盾牌，紧握祖母绿橄榄枝，下方刻有光芒四射的“250”字样，上方刻有 18 克拉黄金蚀刻的“250 YEARS USA”字样。
本周，美国驻比利时大使比尔·怀特 (Bill White) 赠送给美国总统唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 的手表大小的金戒指上镶嵌着 321 颗钻石、56 颗蓝宝石、13 颗祖母绿和 6 颗红宝石。
在布鲁塞尔举行的纪念美国 250 岁生日的活动中，特朗普在预先录制的视频中说道：“我要特别感谢来自安特卫普的朋友们，感谢你们为我设计了宏伟的 Freedom 250 戒指。”
安特卫普世界钻石中心 (AWDC) 总裁伊西多尔·莫塞尔 (Isidore Mörsel) 代表比利时港口城市这座拥有数百年历史的钻石社区赠送了这枚戒指。安特卫普是全球宝石贸易的中心节点，去年在特朗普全面贸易战的重压下陷入困境。
“愿这枚戒指能够永久提醒我们，真正的伙伴关系就像最好的天然钻石一样，是在压力下形成的，经受住时间的考验，并在建立在信任的基础上闪耀出最耀眼的光芒，”莫塞尔说。戒指内部刻有“Crafted in Antwerp for Donald John Trump”字样。
以美元计算，与卡塔尔捐赠的价值 4 亿美元的飞机（特朗普下令将其改装为新的空军一号）等礼物相比，这枚戒指的价值相形见绌。但这是一扇耀眼的窗口，让我们看到那些试图讨好美国总统的人所扮演的炫耀——而且几乎总是镀金的——礼物所扮演的角色。
一位不愿透露姓名的白宫官员周四表示，这枚戒指尚未交给特朗普。
这份礼物是在比利时钻石行业赢得美国取消钻石进口关税几个月后颁发的。9 月，AWDC 表示，它已“成功确保安特卫普每年向美国出口超过 20 亿美元的成品钻石，实行零进口关税”。该组织的发言人周四表示，AWDC 在与特朗普就 2025 年关税问题进行广泛谈判时向欧盟委员会提供了“意见”，但其本身并未游说政府。
美国总统在接受来自国内外的礼物方面有相当大的自由裁量权，并可以自行决定礼物是送给他们个人还是国家。例外情况是来自外国政府的礼物，未经国会同意，宪法中的外国薪酬条款禁止此类礼物，但如果总统希望保留官方礼物，可以使用个人资金向财政部偿还官方礼物的全部价值。
个人礼物也应该登记在总统的年度财务披露中。特朗普本周发布的 2025 年财务披露披露了一件价值 25 万美元的礼物，一件雕塑，描绘了他在 2024 年宾夕法尼亚州巴特勒竞选集会上遭遇暗杀后幸存的胜利姿态，以及 10 项体育赛事的门票，其中包括国际足联詹尼·因凡蒂诺 (Gianni Infantino) 赠送的 10 张即将在新泽西举行的世界杯决赛的门票，价值集体15,000 美元。
四名美国道德专家告诉美联社，特朗普打破了白宫几十年来避免接受此类礼物的惯例。
为了打造这枚戒指，AWDC 向安特卫普的一家高端珠宝商 David Gotlib 求助，他的袖扣售价超过 15,000 欧元（17,000 美元）。
AWDC 和 Gotlib 均未提供这枚戒指的估价，但两家独立珠宝商告诉美联社，他们估计该戒指的价值在 25,000 美元至 35,000 美元之间。
巴黎和伦敦的珠宝顾问 Alexander Levinson 计算出这枚戒指的成本为 25,928 美元，而加拿大第三代奢华珠宝商 David Saad 则将这枚戒指的定价在 33,000 美元至 35,000 美元之间。两人都表示，一半成本来自材料，一半来自劳动力。
戒指在布鲁塞尔星光璀璨的舞台上亮相后，美国联邦调查局局长卡什·帕特尔的女友、音乐家亚历克西斯·威尔金斯向 8,000 多人喝着来自田纳西州和肯塔基州的百威啤酒和波本威士忌，唱起了美国国歌。
怀特大使表示，他从国防工业巨头洛克希德马丁公司和诺斯罗普格鲁曼公司等企业赞助商、英特尔、谷歌和Meta等科技公司以及欧洲巧克力公司列奥尼达斯和费列罗等企业赞助商那里筹集了超过550万美元，用于庆祝250周年纪念活动。AWDC 表示它也提供了资金。
“媒体问，‘为什么一定要这么大？’”怀特谈到这一事件时说道。“因为我们是美利坚合众国！”
这位大使周五在社交媒体上发帖称，他将戒指交给特朗普后，将在椭圆形办公室展示。

item.title: 国际刑警组织公布摩纳哥爆炸案嫌疑人名单，据称袭击目标是与俄罗斯有联系的乌克兰大亨
item.description:
巴黎（美联社）——国际刑警组织周五确认，一名来自乌克兰的 39 岁妇女是摩纳哥爆炸案的主要嫌疑人，据报道，这起袭击的目标是一名与俄罗斯有联系的乌克兰大亨。
该警察组织在红色通缉令中寻求逮捕阿纳斯塔西娅·别列佐夫斯卡（Anastasiia Berezovska），她的罪名是谋杀未遂、意图犯罪、在公共场所放置爆炸装置和共谋犯罪。
摩纳哥当局尚未确认周一公寓楼入口爆炸中受伤的三人身份，但表示他们是一家人，而且似乎是专门针对的目标。
媒体报道称，乌克兰建筑大亨瓦迪姆·耶尔莫拉耶夫（Vadym Yermolaiev）也在伤者之列。他表示，他在近十年前放弃了乌克兰国籍，并因与俄罗斯的关系而在 2023 年成为乌克兰制裁的目标。
一名妇女和一名儿童也受伤。检察官周五表示，其中一名受害者仍处于生命危险之中，并提到了另外两名在袭击中受轻伤的“附带受害者”。
调查人员还在寻求确定嫌疑人是否有同谋或代表他人行事。
摩纳哥副检察官摩根·雷蒙德在新闻发布会上表示：“爆炸装置的相对复杂性和作案手法表明，安置该装置的人并非单独行动。”
他说，这枚炸弹是使用遥控器从远处引爆的，其残骸正在邻国法国进行分析。
这次袭击震惊了摩纳哥，摩纳哥是富人和名人的沿海游乐场，以其税收优惠、王室和一级方程式大奖赛而闻名。其国家元首阿尔贝二世亲王将这次爆炸描述为“令人憎恶的行为”，并表示所有公共服务都已动员起来以确保安全。
检察官尚未给出袭击动机。
国际刑警组织的通知称，嫌疑人的右臂从肩膀到肘部有一个纹身，可能是一条蛇。上面说她出生在乌克兰，有一头黑发，会说德语。
雷蒙德说，嫌疑人最初被确认为一名身材魁梧的男性，穿着深色长袖上衣、浅色短裤和黑色渔夫帽。对前几天的闭路电视录像和目击者的证词进行更广泛的审查后，调查方向转向了一名女扮男装的女子。
国际刑警组织的红色通缉令包括两张照片，其中一名女子穿着带有深色条纹的白色 T 恤，其中一张在街上，她左手拿着似乎是某种电子设备，拖着一根电缆。
作为调查的一部分，两名男性被警方拘留，但随后均被释放。
调查人员还发现了嫌疑人在摩纳哥使用的一辆装有德国车牌的租用车辆。嫌疑人的逃跑路线被追踪，包括从法国到意大利，然后穿越几个欧洲国家到达她的居住国。雷蒙德说，她最后一次为人所知的地址是在德国，“与这个国家的司法合作特别活跃。”
警方和检察官周五在一份声明中表示，包括特种部队在内的德国警方周四搜查了一名 39 岁乌克兰妇女在法兰克福附近租用的公寓，以配合调查。
他们补充说，该妇女使用的车辆也受到了搜查和保护，证据将移交给摩纳哥当局。他们表示，该女子正在逃亡，目前无法透露更多细节。
据信，乌克兰在战争过程中对俄罗斯人物进行了袭击和定点清除，尽管这些袭击大多局限于乌克兰或俄罗斯领土。
2024年12月，乌克兰安全部门声称对杀害俄罗斯军方核生化军事保护部队负责人负责。
西方情报官员最近表示，俄罗斯自2022年2月全面入侵乌克兰以来，加大了定点清除力度。
柏林的 Geir Moulson 对本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 特朗普赞扬美国例外论，然后转向黑暗的政治演讲迎来美国250周年
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters.
“Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty,” he said from Mount Rushmore. “It is the greatest threat to our country, including World War I, World War II, Pearl Harbor or even 9/11.”
While the language wassimilarto several other speeches Trump has given in recent days, it was notable for being delivered in a national park that commemorates some of America’s most prominent presidents. And it swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents like Gerald Ford or Ronald Reagan have delivered during earlier high-profile Independence Day celebrations.
Indeed, Trump’s language evoked the Red Scare of the 1950s, when alleged communists were persecuted and blacklisted from jobs across America, from Washington to Hollywood.
In New York City, Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a democratic socialist, delivered his own address that cast America as a nation of contradictions “working each day towards the perfection in which it was conceived.”
The president’s speech capped an Independence Day eve that was otherwise most notable for a brutal heat wave the gripped much of the eastern portion of the country. Officials have warned those celebrating the holiday to stay hydrated and take air-conditioned breaks as needed.
Philadelphia canceled its Salute to Independence parade Friday. The Great American State Fair in Washington shut down in the early afternoon before reopening at 5 p.m. The Capitol Fourth concert, a mainstay of the holiday in Washington, opened its gates a little later than normal but ultimately moved forward with appearances from Patti LaBelle, Trace Adkins, members of the Artemis II space mission and fireworks over George Washington’s Mount Vernon. An Independence Day parade scheduled for Saturday in Washington was canceled.
By early afternoon Friday in Washington, hundreds of people were roaming the grounds of the National Mall, home to TheGreat American State Fair. They snapped photos of the flyovers and tried to cool off inside tents that offered $9 lemonades and $23 turkey legs. Many were dressed in patriotic colors, their faces glistening with sweat.
New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivers a speech to mark the 250th anniversary of the United States at City Hall on Friday, July 3, 2026 in New York. (Anna Connors/The New York Times via AP, Pool)
Glenn Brooks, who was pardoned by Trump for his participation in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the Capitol, said he was “thankful to be participating in this grand event.”
The activity culminates in the main event Saturday, when fireworks will erupt in communities across the U.S., along with backyard cookouts and block parties. Trump will deliver another speech at the National Mall in Washington before what is being billed as a historically massive fireworks show.
As the rest of the country struggled under stifling heat, the Pacific Northwest enjoyed temperatures in the 60s with even a few light showers.
World Cup soccer fans in Seattle were staying cool Friday as they got psyched up for Monday’s big game between the U.S. and Belgium. In the nearby suburb of Issaquah, Megan Kurowski, 31, brought her two dogs to the dog park so they could get some exercise before she went to work.
Kurowski said she was feeling positive about America’s 250th anniversary and was planning a possible paddleboard to watch the fireworks.
“Everyone’s just, from what it seems, been pretty excited about celebrating 250 years,” she said.
The holiday is unfolding at a unique time in the U.S. The anniversary has served as an opportunity for the country to reflect on its history while also reminding it of the political polarization of the moment.
Visitors walk up the front steps of the Jefferson Memorial while carrying a sun umbrella during an extreme heat advisory, Thursday, July 2, 2026, in Washington. (AP Photo/Nathan Howard)
In New York, Mamdani, a Democrat, did not mention Trump by name, but parts of his speech appeared aimed at the president’s divisive rhetoric.
“For generation after generation, we have been told that when the world has sent its people to our shores, it has not sent its best,” Mamdani said in an apparent reference to a common criticism from Trump. “Those ideals upon which our nation was built — they are strong enough to endure any authoritarian regime, but only if we reach for them.”
Freedom 250, an organization aligned with the White House, has come to rival America250, a bipartisan group founded by Congress a decade ago. Freedom 250 has organized much of the activity in Washington, including theGreat American State Fair. America250 is behind the ball drops unfolding in many cities, including New York, and will host a concert in Los Angeles on Saturday.
About 4 in 10 U.S. adults feel “proud” about the country’s 250th anniversary, according to an AprilsurveyfromThe Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. Roughly 3 in 10 said “excited” describes their emotions.
Runners wearing Independence Day-themed items jog at dawn past an overlook in Arlington, Va., as Washington prepares for sweltering temperatures, Friday, July 3, 2026. (AP Photo/J. Scott Applewhite)
Ahead of the holiday, auto technician Joe Fuqua-Bejarano in Topeka, Kansas, sized up “what makes us awesome” as a people. It is clearly not the politics, in his view, but rather resilience.
“We’ve just all got to find unity somewhere, whether that’s in laughter or perseverance, and keep everybody cool,” he said from the fireworks stand where he is doing a booming business as a side hustle.
Christina Zhou, a 25-year-old research assistant from Cambridge, Massachusetts, said she would aim to “think about just things that are happening locally.”
“It feels a little bit more like within our own personal control,” she said.
Jerry Chin of Newcastle, Washington, said he wasn’t aware that the U.S. was celebrating its 250th anniversary and planned to stay low-key around the holiday. He and his wife generally skip the fireworks and instead stay home with their fearful dogs to keep them calm.
“America’s a great place, but there are some concerns,” he said. Chin, 55, and his wife worry about healthcare and issues around staying healthy, but they also stress about politics.
“We’re Democrats, so kind of given up hope,” he said. “Just feel that it is the way it is. I don’t know if there could be change.”
At the National Archives in Washington, visitors made their way through the Rotunda to look at the Declaration of Independence, the Constitution and the Bill of Rights -- and to escape the heat outside.
Michael Dresdner, 60, traveled from West Orange, New Jersey, with his wife, Cindi, 57, and about two dozen other people to be part of the America 250 celebrations. He said their group of travelers included people on both sides of the political aisle -- and that is what gave him hope for the future of American democracy.
“We are all here, and we all love America,” he said.
Peoples reported from New York. Associated Press writers Martha Bellisle in Seattle, Anthony Izaguirre in New York, John Hanna in Topeka, Kan., Michael Casey in Cambridge, Mass., and Calvin Woodward, Didi Tang, Gary Fields and Nathan Ellgren in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 一名加州农民赠送了大量他不准出售的油桃
item.description:
Thousands of visitors have flocked to Cesar Mora’s farm in central California this week to gather free nectarines.
He’sgiving his harvest awayrather than watching it rot as he’s locked in a legal battle with a company that claims exclusive rights over the variety of white nectarine he grows. He’s shared more than 100,000 pounds (45,359 kilograms) since Monday.
“It was really just a thought of not wasting a perfectly good product,” Mora said. “It does make a grower feel good, being able to share my fruit with people and see their immediate reaction that they love it. It’s a little bit of good in this tough situation that I’ve been dealing with.”
The legal dispute highlights the tension that can emerge between farmers and the plant breeders and large industrial food marketers that create new varieties of plants and obtain the exclusive rights to sell them.
Since 2023, the third-generation farmer in the agricultural community of Reedley in California’s Central Valley has been fighting a lawsuit filed against him by Giumarra Brothers Fruit Co. The suit centers on the company’s claims of exclusivity over a variety of white nectarine and accusations that Mora broke their contract by selling the fruit to other packers. A trial has been scheduled for later this month.
“At its heart, this is a disagreement involving two written agreements, and it is being resolved the right way — in court and on the facts,” the company said in a statement emailed by one of its attorneys.
Mora has accused the company of unfair and fraudulent business practices.
Cesar Mora stands next to bins filled with nectarines as workers pick fruit at his orchard in Reedley, Calif., Wednesday, July 1, 2026, ahead of a free giveaway amid an ongoing contract dispute over the crop. (AP Photo/Jae C. Hong)
The fight centers on a white nectarine variety known as “Monalise,” which has a sweeter, less tart taste.
In its court filings, Giumarra says all rights to the Monalise variety are owned by Star Fruits Diffusion, a French company that works with plant breeding programs, while Giumarra holds the right to sublicense the variety for testing, production and sale. Star Fruits Diffusion did not respond to a request for comment.
Plant breeders, including universities, have long experimented with breeding new crop varieties, and some have become household names. Washington State University developed the Rainier cherry in the 1950s, while the University of Minnesota released the Honeycrisp apple in the 1990s. Both varieties are now in the public domain and can be grown and sold by anyone.
Fruit patents are becoming increasingly common, said Bradley Rickard, professor of food and agricultural economics at Cornell University. A patent allows a breeder to collect a royalty from the fruit trees it sells, the fruit that the trees produce, or both.
In 2010, more than a dozen apple growers sued the University of Minnesota for awarding exclusive rights to its SweeTango apple to an orchard that organized a cooperative of growers to market the variety. A settlement maintained the university’s license agreement with the cooperative while also allowing more Minnesota orchards to lease the trees that bear the apple variety.
Family members and volunteers bag nectarines during a free giveaway at Cesar Mora’s orchard in Reedley, Calif., Wednesday, July 1, 2026, amid an ongoing contract dispute over the crop. (AP Photo/Jae C. Hong)
California’s Central Valley, stretching some 20,000 square miles (51,800 square kilometers), is an agricultural powerhouse that’s estimated to produce 40% of the nation’s fruits, nuts and other table foods, including most of its nectarines.
Court filings show Mora signed a sublicensing agreement with Giumarra in 2017 allowing him to grow and sell the Monalise. He entered a marketing agreement in 2019 requiring the fruit to be packed and sold through Giumarra. He said Giumarra recruited him to grow it.
Under the agreements, Mora was to pay Giumarra a royalty of $2.50 per tree and a 4% production royalty based off the gross sales of the fruit the trees produced, as well as a sales commission.
“They sold me hope and a big dream that I thought I could participate in with them,” he said.
Mora alleges that up to half of the nectarines he provided to Giumarra in 2020 were thrown away, reducing his profits. The company disputes this, and the judge overseeing the case found that the statute of limitations for those claims had passed.
In 2022, Mora alleges the company sold his nectarines to Taiwan in violation of the contract, which states Giumarra will market and sell them in the U.S. and Canada. Giumarra also disputes that claim.
Mora later sought to terminate his relationship with Giumarra, and he sold his nectarines to another fruit packer in 2023. That’s when Giumarra sued him for breach of contract, leading to his inability to sell the fruit at all while the court battle plays out.
Mora’s attorneys say Giumarra has not provided documents regarding its license to the nectarine variety. The company said in court filings the Monalise is not covered by a U.S. plant patent. Mora’s attorneys claim in court filings that “Giumarra promised and represented that the Nectarines were an ‘exclusive variety’ of fruit, and thus Giumarra held patents and related legal rights over these nectarines,” and that because it was exclusive, the fruit “would be sold for top dollar.”
Fresno County Superior Court Judge Jon Skiles in May ruled that Giumarra’s breach of contract claim can go forward, saying that the agreement between Giumarra and Mora is valid whether there is a patent for the fruit or not.
“The sublicense agreement does not expressly state that its validity is dependent on the existence or issuance of a patent for the fruit,” he wrote.
He added that Giumarra “does not have to prove the existence of the underlying license agreement in order to prove that it has a valid contract with defendant regarding growing and selling the fruit.”
Family members and volunteers wearing “No Nectarines Wasted” T-shirts bag nectarines at Cesar Mora’s orchard in Reedley, Calif., Wednesday, July 1, 2026, as part of a free giveaway amid an ongoing contract dispute over the crop. (AP Photo/Jae C. Hong)
Mora said the yearslong litigation has left him feeling frustrated and defenseless. He also grows peaches and plums that are not subject to agreements with Giumarra, but he has lost a quarter of his income by not being able to sell his nectarine crop. Mora says he hopes his case results in more legal protections for growers, while Giumarra says it will let the evidence speak at trial.
“It’s been discouraging to even want to go out and farm,” he said.
On Wednesday, locals wore T-shirts that read “No Nectarines Wasted” as they bagged up the free fruit, with some staying to help Mora serve the large crowds. He’s raised more than $17,000 through a GoFundMe page.
“The only saving grace through all this is being able to share it with the public,” Mora said of his nectarines, “and having everybody enjoy it.”

item.title: 特朗普政府对乔治华盛顿时期奴隶制的解释可以重新采用
item.description:
上诉法院周五裁定，唐纳德·特朗普总统的政府可以在乔治·华盛顿总统位于费城的家中重新安装解释小组，批评者称这些解释小组粉饰了奴隶制的历史。
这些标志位于 1776 年 7 月 4 日通过《独立宣言》的同一地区。周五，国家公园管理局留下了一条寻求评论的信息。
新的教育展板旨在取代 2010 年设立的展板，该展板讲述了 1790 年代费城短暂担任美国首都时，九名奴隶如何与乔治华盛顿和玛莎华盛顿一起生活在家里的故事。
它们的删除源于特朗普的 2025 年行政命令，该命令呼吁联邦拥有或控制的历史遗址不要展示“贬低美国人过去或现在”的信息，并关注“美国人民的伟大成就和进步”。
美国第三巡回上诉法院的三名法官组成的小组周五做出的裁决是一项技术性裁决，允许执行上个月做出的裁决，该裁决位于总统府所在地十字路口对面的法院。
这项裁决由特朗普提名的一名法官、前总统乔治·W·布什提名的一名法官和前总统巴拉克·奥巴马选出的一名法官做出，称下级法院强迫联邦政府撤销其新专家组的做法是错误的。
政府周四请求批准将它们重新安装，并表示面板已准备好安装，并且应该“不再拖延”。政府在法庭文件中表示，其信息还讨论了奴隶制。
几个月来，支持者、学者和官员一直担心，符合特朗普命令的版本可能会淡化这个国家过去的痛苦，转而支持一种更加胜利的观点。
政府网站上的新面板图像显示，他们仍然可以获得有关住在家里的被奴役者的信息，以及废奴运动的详细信息、宪法如何对待奴隶制、宾夕法尼亚州奴隶制的终结以及华盛顿和他的继任者约翰·亚当斯如何看待和对待奴隶制，以及有关 20 世纪民权运动的信息。替换面板不包含早期面板中的一些细节，例如奴隶贸易路线地图和奴隶制时间表。他们还避免出现“奴隶制的肮脏行径”等批评性头条新闻。
费城市因删除之前的信息而提起诉讼，并试图阻止新安装。该市周五要求上诉法院撤销当天早些时候的命令——至少足够长的时间，以便该市能够回应特朗普政府周四提出的要求。
费城在文件中表示，如果新的面板重新启用，将会受到伤害：“总统府对于费城和国家来说是一个极其重要的场所，它是通过多年的联邦与地方合作而发展起来的，讲述了一个具有历史意义且长期被压抑的故事。”
今年早些时候，大约一半的旧面板被重新安装，随后法院下令停止这项工作。

item.title: 特朗普在乌莎·万斯的播客上读了一本儿童读物，然后即兴谈论了前任总统和他自己
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——美国总统唐纳德·特朗普和第二夫人乌莎·万斯一起参加了她的播客，嘉宾们给孩子们读图画书，但因偏离剧本而臭名昭著的特朗普对他的前任、他的体格以及他在白宫的生活方式进行了连续评论。
特朗普在周五在线发布的万斯“与第二夫人的故事时间”播客中露面时，总统读到了“总统玩！”白宫历史协会的一本儿童读物，其中有总统享受体育运动以及使用白宫及其休闲场所的插图。
特朗普为六月中旬在白宫椭圆形办公室的露面做好了准备，他在白宫椭圆形办公室添加了丰富的金色装饰，并补充了秃鹰毛绒动物、由成堆超大书籍组成的桌子和用乐高积木制成的地球仪。
当万斯问特朗普在担任总统期间是否有很多时间读书消遣时，他回答说他最终主要是看报纸。
“我通常会读关于我自己的故事，”特朗普说。
当特朗普翻阅这本儿童读物时，他对过去的总统进行了观察，讲了一些笑话，并为他在白宫建造的宽敞宴会厅插上了插头。
他形容林登·约翰逊是个“强硬的人”，罗纳德·里根是一个“高素质的人”，“就像你的父亲是总统一样”，而约翰·F·肯尼迪是“第二好看的总统”。没有说的是特朗普认为谁是最好看的总统。
理查德·尼克松是唯一一位在卷入水门丑闻后辞职的总统，“我猜他自己陷入了麻烦”。书中描绘了大萧条时期的总统​​赫伯特·胡佛正在玩一个他编造的名为“胡佛球”的游戏。
特朗普打趣道：“这对他来说比对经济更有利。”
巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)长期以来一直是特朗普嘲笑的对象，图中他正在打篮球。
特朗普称他为“巴拉克·侯赛因·奥巴马”，并表示他怀疑奥巴马是否是一名优秀的篮球运动员。然后他分享说奥巴马最喜欢的运动是高尔夫，但补充说，“他不会很快参加大师赛”，指的是职业高尔夫锦标赛。
当他翻到一张画着比尔·克林顿在这位前总统在白宫安装的慢跑跑道上跑步的页面时，特朗普说：“我认为我永远不会这样做。”
但他补充说，他“非常喜欢”克林顿。
特朗普在看到亚伯拉罕·林肯骑马的照片后萌生了骑马的念头。
“那太好了。我也想骑马，”特朗普说。“事实上，这给了我一个想法，但是当你从马上摔下来时……我见过太多的事情发生了。从马上摔下来可不好。”
他建议的解决方案是“一匹漂亮的老马，速度极慢，懒惰”，他“也许会骑它”。
约翰·昆西·亚当斯 (John Quincy Adams) 在当时流经白宫南草坪的台伯河 (Tiber Creek) 里游泳的照片促使特朗普说道，“我认为我们正在上面建造一个美丽的舞厅。”
其他总统的一些身体活动引发了人们对他自己体质的一些反思。
当看到杰拉尔德·福特在泳池里游泳时，特朗普说：“我不知道我穿泳衣好看不好看。我已经很久没有穿泳衣了。”
威廉·霍华德·塔夫脱以腰围闻名，“是我们最重的总统，”特朗普说。
“我必须小心，因为我不想取代他的记录，”特朗普说。“如果我允许这种事情发生，这样的事情就有可能发生。在场的所有人，请保持良好的状态。”
除了鼓励年轻观众保持体形之外，当第二夫人向特朗普询问为什么他们应该在 7 月 4 日庆祝国家时，特朗普还向他提供了一个有点模糊的信息。
“我们有一个伟大的国家，”特朗普说。“我们有一个国家，现在正处于一个边缘。它可以朝这个方向发展，也可以朝另一个方向发展，你明白这一点。但我们会让它朝另一个方向发展。我们将使美国比以往任何时候都更伟大。”

item.title: 特朗普赦免阿布拉莫夫前合伙人，9人因违反汽车排放管制而被定罪
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——周五，唐纳德·特朗普总统赦免了 11 人，其中包括华盛顿说客杰克·阿布拉莫夫的前商业伙伴，以及白宫认定的 9 名帮助人们绕过车辆排放控制系统的人。
特赦令出台之际，特朗普在第二个任期内发布了一系列赦免令，特别是对盟友、公众人物和那些被视为政治上一致的人。
他利用总统职位的广泛能力单方面给予赦免和减刑，这是共和党重新执政后广泛使用行政权力的方式之一。
特朗普周五早些时候在社交媒体上宣布了一些赦免令，但没有透露任何赦免者的姓名。
特朗普在他的真相社交媒体网络上写道：“我非常荣幸地刚刚为六名因‘修理汽车’而受到拜登政府迫害、入狱或被送进监狱的人签署了赦免令。”
“我现在就让他们全部自由！”他说。
在白宫周五晚上提供的一份名单中，特朗普赦免了 11 人，其中 9 人因禁用车辆排放监测系统或出售绕过排放系统的设备而面临违反《清洁空气法》的指控。
特朗普周一签署了一份备忘录，告诉美国环境保护局，美国人可以根据自己的需要修理自己的车辆，随后进行了赦免。特朗普在签署备忘录时提到了他去年赦免的一名柴油机械师，该机械师禁用了排放监测系统。
该备忘录还涉及售后汽车零部件，并将取代加州空气资源委员会评估影响车辆排放的零部件的能力。
白宫在公布赦免名单时称特朗普“让消费者摆脱了这些监管负担”。
除了与排放相关的赦免外，特朗普周五还赦免了阿布拉莫夫的前商业伙伴亚当·基丹。
基丹于2005年承认犯有与购买一批赌船有关的欺诈和共谋罪，并于2006年被判处近六年监禁。
此案是对 2000 年代初涉及阿布拉莫夫、国会山、内政部和乔治·W·布什总统政府成员的游说丑闻进行更广泛调查的一部分。
白宫表示，2009 年出狱后，基丹开始在一家人才中介机构工作，随后创立了人才派遣公司 Chartwell Staffing Solutions，现在担任 Empire Workforce Solutions 的总裁。
今年三月，《新闻日报》报道称，基丹是特朗普海湖庄园为长岛共和党国会候选人筹款活动的主持人之一。
周五晚上，发送给基丹公司寻求评论的消息没有立即得到回复。
特朗普周五还赦免了牧场主杰克·哈佛，理由是他在定罪后有着“良好的记录”，并赞扬他允许美国军队和北约部队在他的土地上免费训练。
白宫没有立即公布有关哈佛的更多细节，包括他的定罪。

item.title: 埃及队点球大战4-2战胜澳大利亚队首次晋级世界杯淘汰赛
item.description:
得克萨斯州阿灵顿（美联社）——无论穆罕默德·萨拉赫对自己在埃及足球的未来做出什么决定——是否参加更多世界杯——这位明星前锋可以说，他是埃及队在淘汰赛中首场胜利的队长。
霍萨姆·阿卜杜勒马吉德在点球大战中攻入决定性进球，埃及队在32强赛1-1战平后，通过点球大战以4-2击败澳大利亚队，首次亮相世界杯淘汰赛。
澳大利亚门将马修·瑞安在加时赛末段替换首发帕特里克·比奇后，没有阻止埃及队的四次射门。
这场胜利是埃及第四次参加世界杯，这也是埃及首次在扩大到48人的情况下举行世界杯。澳大利亚目前在淘汰赛中0-3领先。
埃及队接下来将在亚特兰大举行的 16 强赛中迎战卫冕冠军阿根廷队，后者在周五晚上的加时赛中以 3-2 击败佛得角。
“我今天的感觉是，这太不可思议了，”34 岁的前利物浦球员萨拉赫说道，他比国家队教练霍萨姆·哈桑 (Hossam Hassan) 创下的埃及国家队 69 粒进球纪录还差一球。“我总是喜欢看到孩子们快乐并享受这一刻。没有什么能比得上。所以今天是我一生中最美好的日子之一。”
澳大利亚队的哈里·苏塔尔 (Harry Souttar) 在点球大战中错失了射门，而 18 岁的卢卡斯·赫林顿 (Lucas Herrington) 第四次尝试击中横梁，为法老队的阿卜杜勒马吉德 (Abdelmaguid) 奠定了决定性的一球。
25 岁的阿卜杜勒马吉德在 15 场国际比赛中没有进球，当瑞安跳到后卫右侧时，他向左低射，在座无虚席的达拉斯牛仔队主场 70,244 名球迷中引发了一场喧闹的庆祝活动，其中很多人都穿着埃及红球衣。
不到两周前，埃及队在小组赛中以3-1击败新西兰队，之前在世界杯上还没有取得过一场胜利。
“我只想到埃及球迷，”哈桑通过翻译说道。“在整个时间和点球大战期间，我只是祈祷，‘上帝，请让埃及人民幸福。’说实话，甚至在点球大战之前也是如此。”
埃及队的其他点球得分手是马哈茂德·萨贝尔、拉米·拉比亚和萨拉赫，尽管在小组赛决赛中腿筋受伤，他们还是打满了常规时间和加时赛的每一分钟。
杰克逊·欧文和艾维尔·马比尔在点球大战中为澳大利亚队进球。
“当我去找球员并与他们交谈时，我想减轻一些压力，”哈桑说。“不要看压力。把一切都发泄出来，不要想任何事情。想想你的点球。甚至不要考虑守门员。只想想你的踢球。”
埃玛姆·阿舒尔 (Emam Ashour) 在第 13 分钟的头球破门，帮助埃及在常规赛中取得领先。澳大利亚队在第55场扳平了比分，埃及后卫穆罕默德·哈尼成为第一位在同一届世界杯上打进两个乌龙球的球员。
艾登·奥尼尔在禁区左侧开出任意球，哈尼头球攻破门将穆斯塔法·舒比尔，造成了乌龙球，创造了世界杯耻辱的纪录。他的另一个乌龙球是在小组赛1-1战平比利时的比赛中打进的。
不到10分钟前，哈尼在澳大利亚中场的头球尝试中与康纳·梅特卡夫相撞后在同一地点附近倒地。医务人员用担架在附近照顾哈尼，但他被扶了起来，并在接受了脑震荡检查后留下来。
澳大利亚队在世界杯淘汰赛中唯一的进球是两个乌龙球。2006年澳大利亚队0-1负于意大利队，四年前在卡塔尔以1-2负于阿根廷队。对阵阿根廷队的进球是乌龙球。
“当你距离那么近时会很痛，”澳大利亚队教练托尼·波波维奇说。“不幸的是，我们在点球大战中出局，所以现在很难接受。”
下半场开场，奥马尔·马尔穆什本有绝佳机会帮助埃及取得两球领先，但他的射门偏出。
尽管22岁的比奇多次大力扑救，这位已故门将还是换上了34岁的瑞安，这是他第105次参加国家队比赛，而22岁的比奇只为澳大利亚队打了第六场比赛。
在比赛的最后时刻，比奇对拉比亚的头球进行了大力扑救，几秒钟后，萨拉赫的射门就被轻松扑出。
常规赛结束前，埃及队还有一次机会，但海塞姆·哈桑被苏塔尔挡出，苏塔尔用膝盖挡住了射门。

item.title: 特拉维斯·凯尔斯 (Travis Kelce) 的队友和其他体育界人士参加了他与泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 的婚礼
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——朱朱·史密斯-舒斯特也在场。刚刚赢得超级碗冠军的库珀·库普也是如此。
周五，特拉维斯·凯尔斯 (Travis Kelce) 和泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 在麦迪逊广场花园举行的婚礼，明星云集，其中包括 NFL 球员和其他著名体育人物。
卡里姆·亨特是凯尔斯在婚礼前在纽约现身的众多堪萨斯城酋长队现任或前任队友之一，预计出席婚礼的还有凯尔斯退役的费城老鹰队超级碗冠军兄弟杰森、旧金山 49 人队近端锋乔治·基特尔以及其他来自橄榄球、高尔夫等领域的人士。
足球甚至也出现了，不过是以《泰德·拉索》演员杰森·苏戴奇斯的形式出现的，他本人就是酋长队的忠实粉丝。
婚礼在 NBA 纽约尼克斯队和 NHL 流浪者队的主场举行。斯威夫特坐在场边观看了 NBA 总决赛第四场比赛，尼克斯队在落后 29 分的情况下逆转击败马刺队，完成了联盟历史上季后赛该阶段最大的逆转。
自 1968 年在宾州车站顶上的当前版本开业以来，MSG 一直是一些标志性体育时刻的举办地。
1970 年，尼克斯队在那里赢得了他们的第一个 NBA 冠军，当时威利斯·里德 (Willis Reed) 伤愈复出，并在第七场比赛中战胜了洛杉矶湖人队 (Los Angeles Lakes)。
1971 年，乔·弗雷泽 (Joe Frazier) 在两位传奇人物三场拳击比赛中的第一场比赛中击败了穆罕默德·阿里 (Muhammad Ali)，这场比赛被称为“世纪之战”。阿里在 74 年的复赛中获胜。
1994年，流浪者队在花园球场击败温哥华加人队夺得斯坦利杯，结束了长达54年的冠军荒。
凯尔斯是堪萨斯城队三届超级碗冠军，并四次入选美联社全职业球员。杰森曾七次入选全职业中锋，帮助费城队赢得 2017 赛季超级碗冠军。

item.title: 这个高科技球见证了世界杯历史上最戏剧性的时刻之一
item.description:
亚特兰大（美联社）——这被称为“连球技术”。这也是世界杯比赛史上最戏剧性的高潮之一。
由于检测到肉眼甚至视频重播都无法察觉的触碰，克罗地亚在补时阶段对阵葡萄牙的比赛中最终扳平比分的进球被取消。
葡萄牙队在多伦多以 2-1 获胜并晋级 16 强，这让克罗地亚球员和球迷感到震惊，他们认为乔斯科·格瓦尔迪奥尔的进球被 VAR 和裁判埃斯彭·埃斯科斯错误地判为越位。
国际足联依靠的是配备“先进传感器”的高科技足球，并坚称在确定克罗地亚球员伊戈尔·曼塔诺维奇的头部受到了最轻微的目光时，它是正确的，这意味着马里奥·帕拉西奇在进球过程中处于越位位置。
国际足联表示，球内传感器经过精心调整，“能够确定任何轻微的接触……使官员能够获得前所未有的数据水平，从而做出快速、准确的决定。”
由阿迪达斯制造的官方“Trionda”世界杯用球配备了“小型惯性测量单元（IMU）传感器”，国际足联称该传感器的工作频率约为 500Hz，每秒捕获数据 500 次。
国际足联表示，它可以在三个维度上跟踪球的加速度和颗粒运动，并可以检测球员接触的确切时刻。
该球技术与体育场内摄像机相结合，用于跟踪数据，并将数据实时传输到视频助理。
除了帮助确定越位之外，触摸数据还可以用于手球和点球等事件。
这一判罚之所以如此有争议，是因为即使是从多个角度进行慢动作回放，在视觉上也无法确定曼塔诺维奇是否接触到了伊万·佩里西奇的内旋传中。
这就是对技术的依赖。VAR 指示裁判 Eskås 查看边线监视器。回放显示了国际足联所说的“心跳图形”，表明球被触碰的那一刻，并且明显擦过曼塔诺维奇的头部时出现了明显的尖峰。
佐治亚理工学院电气与计算机工程学院教授马诺斯·坦泽里斯 (Manos Tentzeris) 告诉美联社：“无论球移动多快或多旋转，你都可以真正有效地跟踪它。”“球的位置准确度达到 99.99%……你可以清楚地知道球员的位置，甚至是鞋尖，这有时会决定某人是否越位或越位。”
国际足联还在 2022 年世界杯上使用了联网球技术，并在最近的 2024 年男子欧洲锦标赛上部署了该技术。
该技术从 2020-22 年开始进行测试，并在阿拉伯杯和世俱杯等赛事中进行了试用。
球传感器在 2024 年欧洲杯上也产生了决定性的影响，它检测到丹麦队的约阿希姆·安德森 (Joachim Andersen) 在对阵东道主德国队的比赛中在禁区内的处理情况。经过VAR审核后判罚点球，凯·哈弗茨进球，德国2-0获胜。
“在我看来，这不是足球应该有的样子，”丹麦队主教练卡斯帕·胡尔曼德在他的球队也有一个进球被VAR判定无效后说道。
在球队痛苦出局后，克罗地亚队主教练兹拉特科·达利奇也表达了同样的观点。
“所有这些决定都剥夺了足球的乐趣，”他说。
亚特兰大的美联社体育记者毛拉·凯里 (Maura Carey) 也做出了贡献。
詹姆斯·罗布森 (James Robson) 位于 https://x.com/jamesalanrobson

item.title: 泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯结婚了。关于婚礼需要了解什么
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——你的英语老师和体育老师结婚了。我们终于知道了有关泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯在麦迪逊广场花园举行婚礼的更多细节。
亚当·桑德勒主持了这一仪式，这让那些一直在猜测谁将获得这一崇高荣誉的人既高兴又困惑。斯威夫特的兄弟奥斯汀·斯威夫特担任她的伴郎，凯尔斯的兄弟兼播客联合主持人杰森·凯尔斯担任新郎的伴郎。
数百名名人、运动员以及亲密的朋友和家人参加了这次活动，但即使在周五晚上宣布结婚后，许多婚礼细节仍然被隐藏，因为照片仍然难以捉摸——包括斯威夫特或凯尔斯穿着的任何暗示。
以下是我们所知道的（以及仍然不知道的）：
斯威夫特曾开玩笑说，任何与她交谈过的人都会被邀请参加婚礼，而周五的活动似乎兑现了这一承诺。
嘉宾包括歌手卡米拉·卡贝洛、演员休·格兰特、伊桑·霍克和杰森·苏戴奇斯、模特卡莉·克劳斯和吉吉·哈迪德，还有纽约巨人队外接手朱朱·史密斯-舒斯特、西雅图海鹰队外接手库珀·库普和旧金山 49 人队近端锋乔治·基特尔。
列出那些没有出席的人可能更容易，其中显然包括布莱克·莱弗利和瑞安·雷诺兹，他们此前与斯威夫特关系密切，但据报道在假期周末出现在纽约州北部。
佐伊·克拉维茨 (Zoe Kravitz) 出席了活动，但她的未婚妻哈里·斯泰尔斯 (Harry Styles)（斯威夫特的前男友）在巡演期间在伦敦。
MSG 可能提供了斯威夫特和凯尔斯在筹划婚礼时所寻求的隐私和保密，但这也意味着那些渴望了解这对夫妇如何将竞技场改造成婚礼场地的人只能在周五晚上等待。
值得注意的是，尽管宾客名单高达 1,000 人，但截至周五还没有发布或分享任何照片。
尽管许多名人和其他出席者分享了他们在这个重要日子的着装，但目前仍不清楚斯威夫特或凯尔斯穿着什么。
我们唯一知道的是，他们的服装来自 Christian Dior Haute Couture 及其设计师 Jonathan Anderson，鞋子则由 Christian Louboutin 定制。斯威夫特佩戴卡地亚珠宝。
克里斯汀·迪奥高级定制时装的发言人证实，这些婚纱是由安德森设计的，并称它们是“与这对夫妇密切合作”在巴黎创作的。
帝国大厦表示，它亮起蓝色灯光，作为斯威夫特在这个重要日子里的“蓝色之物”。埃尔莫发布了一首诗，祝贺他的朋友们说“我愿意”。这位全球巨星和她的明星足球运动员丈夫的婚姻很快就得到了热烈而欢乐的反应。
多莉·帕顿在婚礼前开玩笑地表示，她想继承他们第一个孩子的权利，同时唱着她著名的歌词“我将永远爱你”。本周早些时候，这对夫妇向帕顿想象力图书馆捐赠了 200 万美元，作为向美国各地和国家慈善机构捐赠 2600 万美元的一部分。

item.title: 从《邀请》到《工作室》，塞斯·罗根喜欢对奥利维亚·王尔德尖叫
item.description:
Seth Rogen gaveOlivia Wildea note at the end of the shoot for“The Invite.”In it, he wrote, “I love screaming at you.”
It wasn’t some toxic jab from a volatile actor to his director and co-star after a difficult shoot; They really have found a kind of magic in screaming at each other on screen, first in the Chinatown-ish “Missing Reel” episode of“The Studio,”in which Wilde plays a satirical version of herself as the crazed director, and then as a couple on the rocks in the acerbic chamber dramedy “The Invite,” whichexpands nationwide on July 10.
“There’s nothing precious about either of us really,” Wilde said. “There was, like, a permission speak — permission to scream — freely.”
Rogen, 44, and Wilde, 42, came up in the same class of sorts, with memorable roles on television, in the late 90s and early-aughts, that blossomed into movie stardom and, eventually, directing. But until recently, they’d only ever really crossed paths meaningfully once: At a table read forJudd Apatow’sslacker pregnancy comedy “Knocked Up” over 20 years ago. Wilde did not get the role, and they went their separate ways.
And perhaps it was for the best. “Knocked Up” might not have been the right use of Wilde and Rogen together. They seem to excel not as a traditional romantic comedy duo, but in a realm that’s more prickly, more abrasive.
In “The Invite,” tensions are already simmering between Joe (Rogen) and Angela (Wilde) when their more liberated upstairs neighbors, neighbors Pína (Penélope Cruz) and Hawk (Edward Norton), arrive for dinner, and stir things up over one booze, and a revelation-filled night.
Wilde and Rogen spoke to The Associated Press about how the cast got Wilde to act in the movie, studio productions and not taking casting too personally. Remarks have been edited for clarity and brevity.
WILDE: I loved the opportunity on “The Studio” to make fun of myself, like what a thrill, and to just make fun of this business in the most loving way. I loved getting to play in that world, but there was no part of me that was like, wait a minute, do you think I’m (expletive) insane?
WILDE: Did you feel offended that I wanted you to be a sexless dad?
ROGEN: A sexless sad man? No, I got it. I give that: Sexless sad dad.
WILDE: This role was like the best of Albert Brooks and a little bit of a ‘70s Richard Dreyfuss or maybe like ’80s Richard Dreyfuss. And I felt like there is a bit of Albert Brooks and Richard Dreyfuss in you, which is high praise.
ROGEN: Very much so. We overtly pressured her to do it. We had a side text chain between us where we would strategize about how to do it and how to launch a multipronged attack on her to back her into doing it. Thank God it worked. And I just kept saying no to every other option she put on the table. I literally made it impossible to hire another person because everyone else you suggested I was like, “I don’t think they’d be good at this.”
WILDE: It did take encouraging because I just for whatever reason had real impostor syndrome about it because I just revere them so much. I felt capable of directing this and holding it all in place and I really felt a strong connection to the story and everything that needed to happen to make it good but the idea of jumping in the ring was intimidating. I never would have suggested myself. I’d rather die than be like, “What if it was me, you guys?”
ROGEN: I’m the exact opposite, I’m always like, “We need a tighter shot of me, I don’t think we’re featuring me enough in all this.”
WILDE: It was the best experience of my career for every reason, but certainly as an actor. I’ve never had that much fun acting. And it made me think that maybe I don’t hate it.
WILDE: I think so. I think that there’s an attitude about marriage in American culture that is very much very reflective of our like, can do attitude, like you’re gonna do it and you’re just gonna stick to it and you’re going to grin and bear it. There’s an American energy to that that I think is part of what keeps people in really rough places and relationships for a long time and in this version of the story, in our version, these people, if they hadn’t been confronted with this evening that the movie’s about, would have just stayed in this kind of difficult conflict-ridden kind of passive-aggressive zone probably forever because they just aren’t people who like to give up. I also think there’s an American puritanical attitude toward sex in general that we are definitely dealing with in the movie, because it’s about people who haven’t talked about sex with each other in a way that they have desperately needed to for years.
ROGEN: Felt American to me, as a Canadian, the most sex-liberal people on the planet.
ROGEN: They would have been way more stressed out that the movie was completely rewritten in the weeks leading to shooting. They don’t like that much.
WILDE: Working for a studio, you don’t get the opportunity to be as — this is like the most obvious statement on the planet — but typically, unless you’rePaul Thomas Anderson, I feel like you don’t get the opportunity to be as specific and, in some ways, hopefully a little bold. I think that we wouldn’t have had the same amount of creative ownership as a group and that was what made the experience so valuable. I’m very happy this wasn’t like a hundred billion dollar Sony movie.
ROGEN: We could have done it. We could find a way to spend it. You give me a budget, I will blow through it.

item.title: 卡罗维发利捷克电影节纪念达斯汀·霍夫曼和朱丽叶·比诺什
item.description:
布拉格（美联社）——捷克共和国卡罗维发利国际电影节拉开了第 60 届的序幕，演员达斯汀·霍夫曼和朱丽叶·比诺什获得了荣誉。
霍夫曼因在《克莱默对克莱默》（1979年）和《雨人》（1988年）中的表演而获得奥斯卡最佳男主角奖，他将在周五的开幕之夜因其对世界电影的杰出艺术贡献而受到表彰。
周六，霍夫曼将放映他 1967 年拍摄的大片《毕业生》，该片为他赢得了首次奥斯卡金像奖提名。
比诺什曾凭借《英国病人》（1996）获得奥斯卡最佳女配角奖和其他多项奖项，她将在 7 月 11 日的闭幕式上获得与霍夫曼相同的奖项。
该电影节将放映她的三部电影：《认证副本》（2010 年）、《三色：蓝色》（1993 年）和《In-I in Motion》（2025 年）。
美国电影摄影师罗伯特·理查森因为奥利弗·斯通、马丁·斯科塞斯和昆汀·塔伦蒂诺导演的作品而闻名，他将成为本届电影节第三位获奖嘉宾。
三届奥斯卡最佳摄影奖得主理查森将于周六推出他的纪录片《罗伯特·理查森：白魔》。
该电影节在捷克西部温泉小镇卡罗维发利举行，评审团将评选 12 部电影作为最高奖项——水晶球奖。

item.title: 道琼斯指数创下新高后，随着一些人工智能股票反弹，世界股市涨跌互现
item.description:
曼谷（美联社）——周五道琼斯工业平均指数再创纪录后，世界股市涨跌互现，一些关键的人工智能相关股票上涨，而其他股票则扩大跌幅。
标准普尔 500 指数未来上涨 0.3%，而道琼斯指数则下跌 0.2%。美国市场将于周五因独立日假期休市。
在欧洲交易中，德国 DAX 指数上涨 0.4%，至 25,667.73 点；巴黎 CAC 40 指数小幅下跌 0.1%，至 8,471.19 点。英国富时 100 指数下跌 0.4%，至 10,613.55 点。
在亚洲，韩国综合指数周四下跌近 8%，但上涨 5.8%，至 8,088.34 点。该国最大的公司和主要计算机芯片制造商三星电子股价上涨 8.2%，而规模较小的竞争对手 SK 海力士股价上涨 10.9%。
在东京，日经 225 指数上涨 1.5%，至 69,744.07 点。芯片制造商 Tokyo Electron 上涨 0.4%，内存制造商 Kioxia 上涨 9.2%。
香港恒生指数上涨 1.3%，至 23,350.03 点；上证综合指数上涨 0.4%，至 4,043.64 点。
台湾 Taix 指数小幅上涨 0.1%，印度 Sensex 指数上涨 0.4%。
在澳大利亚，S&P/ASX 200 指数上涨 1.4%，至 8,844.40 点。
SPI 资产管理公司的史蒂芬·英尼斯 (Stephen Innes) 在评论中表示：“亚洲股市在经历了两次以科技股为主导的交易日后找到了一些立足点，韩国市场再次表明，当每个人都以同样的方式倾斜时，拉长的橡皮筋能够以多么快的速度恢复。”
周四，美国股市多数上涨，道指再创新高，上涨 1.1% 至 52,900.07 点。
计算机芯片公司和人工智能繁荣的其他赢家的股价下跌使指数涨跌互现。
标准普尔 500 指数当天收盘几乎没有变化，涨幅不到 0.1%，尽管该指数内每 10 只股票中有 7 只上涨。收于 7,483.24。
纳斯达克综合指数下跌 0.8%，至 25,382.67 点。
一份显示美国经济前景的报告总体上给股市带来了一些帮助。上个月雇主增加了 57,000 个工作岗位。这对经济有利，但也低于经济学家预期的 10 万个就业岗位，而且 5 月份的招聘速度有所放缓。
弱于预期的结果可能会缓解通胀压力，由于与伊朗的战争导致油价上涨，全球通胀压力一直在加速。现在油价已回到战前水平以下，如果未来几个月通胀放缓，美联储可能会觉得今年不太需要多次加息。
这对投资者来说是一个福音，他们往往喜欢较低的利率，因为这使得美国家庭和企业的借贷和消费成本更低。较低的利率也往往会推高股票和其他投资的价格。
比特币价格在跌至 2024 年以来的最低水平后一天上涨约 2%，加密行业公司的股票也表现强劲。Robinhood Markets 上涨 3.8%，Coinbase Global 上涨 3.9%。周五早些时候，比特币上涨 0.9%。
计算机芯片公司股票的抛售令指数承压。他们面临着压力，因为担心他们的股价在人工智能的狂热中飙升得太高，并且在芯片和数据中心上的所有支出可能不会产生预期的利润和生产力增长。
内存制造商美光科技 (Micron Technology) 回吐早盘涨幅，下跌 5.5%，此前一天暴跌 10.6%。Nvidia 下跌 1.4%，Lam Research 下跌 10.2%。它们是标准普尔 500 指数中权重最大的股票之一，因为它们的规模在人工智能狂热中变得如此巨大。
Nvidia 的总价值接近 4.7 万亿美元，因此其股票走势对标准普尔 500 指数的影响力比其他任何公司都大。
在周五早盘的其他交易中，国际标准布伦特原油下跌不到 0.1%，至每桶 71.76 美元。美国基准原油价格下跌 0.2%，至每桶 68.48 美元。
美元兑日元汇率从161.11日元升至161.14日元。欧元兑美元汇率从 1.1431 美元升至 1.1451 美元。
